title: A patient presented with shortness of breath. His examination showed early diastolic murmur increasing with leaning forw…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
A patient presented with shortness of breath. His examination showed early diastolic murmur increasing with leaning forward. What is the most likely diagnosis? 

MR. 

MS. 

AR. 

AS.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
he government wishes to raise its population’s awareness and knowledge regarding various common topics of the community. How can this be achieved? 

Survey. 

Health education.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
A pregnant lady came to you in her first antenatal visit. Which of the following vaccines can you give her? 

Varicella. 

Rubella. 

Influenza. 

 TDAP

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
A child came suffering from a toxic dose of paracetamol. The physician requests an acetaminophen level. The lab technician calls to report the results. He says: “two” stops and pauses then says: “one, three”. The nurse mistakenly writes it as “2.13” while the real results was “213”. The patient went into irreversible liver damage. What was wrong? 

Poor communication. 

Nurse who failed to repeat the result and double checked that she got the accurate result. 

Lab technician who didn’t repeat or double check that the nurse heard him and recorded correctly.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: All of the above (Poor communication, Nurse who failed to repeat the result and double-checked that she got the accurate result, and Lab technician who didn't repeat or double-check that the nurse heard him and recorded correctly).

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
A patient diagnosed with non-hodgkin lymphoma on current medication. He developed tumor lysis syndrome. What is the expected electrolyte disturbance? 

Hypokalemia, hypocalcemia. 

Hypokalemia, hypercalcemia. 

Hyperkalemia, hypocalcemia. 

Hyperkalemia, hypercalcemia.
